title: Figure 3 from: Ivanov H, Linev A, Pochileev I, Stoyanova V (2026) Droplet digital PCR outperforms qPCR by revealing clinically relevant low-frequency KRAS mutations in colorectal cancer. Pharmacia 73: 1-7. https://doi.org/10.3897/pharmacia.73.e187140
author: Ivanov, Hristo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 3 ddPCR fluorescence amplitude plot showing multiple subclonal mutations. Representative ddPCR 2D amplitude plot demonstrating well-separated fluorescence clusters corresponding to wild-type droplets and distinct KRAS mutant populations in the same sample. This visualization highlights ddPCR's ability to detect co-existing subclonal variants, which are not resolved by qPCR.
steps_raw: Figure 3 ddPCR fluorescence amplitude plot showing multiple subclonal mutations. Representative ddPCR 2D amplitude plot demonstrating well-separated fluorescence clusters corresponding to wild-type droplets and distinct KRAS mutant populations in the same sample. This visualization highlights ddPCR's ability to detect co-existing subclonal variants, which are not resolved by qPCR.
